seoTitle: Mount Annan vs Mount Arthur — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Annan and Mount Arthur. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Annan vs Mount Arthur: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Annan and Mount Arthur.
heading: Mount Annan vs Mount Arthur — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Annan, New South Wales and Mount Arthur, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Annan has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Mount Arthur (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Annan or Mount Arthur safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Annan has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Mount Arthur scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Annan scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Arthur scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Annan or Mount Arthur?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Annan has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
